title: neoholstia tenuifolia var. glabrata
intro: Neoholstia tenuifolia var. glabrata, also known as the Asian holly grape or holly grape, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Chinese and Indian Ayurvedic practices, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Neoholstia tenuifolia var. glabrata include the relief of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by boiling the roots and leaves of the plant in water or extracting its active compounds using solvents.
